The '''cessative''' aspect in Aelaki expresses the cessation or stopping of an action or state. It is one of the [[Stative aspects|stative verb aspects]], formed with the prefix '''nï-''' combined with '''umlaut''' of the verb stem.

== Formation ==

The cessative requires two modifications:
# The prefix '''nï-''' is added to the verb
# The stem undergoes '''umlaut''' (vowel fronting)

{| class="wikitable"
! Stative verb !! Cessative !! Meaning
|-
| zoduk (know) || nïzedik || forget (stop knowing)
|-
| apafath (fall) || nï'æpæfath || stop falling
|}

Note that the stem ''zoduk'' becomes ''zedik'' through umlaut, and ''apafath'' becomes ''æpæfath''.

== Continuative form ==

The continuative of the cessative is formed by reduplicating the prefix:

{| class="wikitable"
! Base !! Continuative !! Meaning
|-
| nïzedik || nïnïzedik || forgetting (in the process of stopping knowing)
|}

== Semantic scope ==

The cessative indicates the end or termination of a state. With ''zoduk'' "know", the cessative ''nïzedik'' means "forget" -- that is, to stop knowing something. The umlaut of the stem reinforces the change-of-state meaning.

== Relationship to other stative aspects ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Prefix !! Aspect !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| ho- || [[Probable]] || hozoduk || curious (about to know)
|-
| nü- || [[Inchoative]] || nüzoduk || learn (start to know)
|-
| '''nï-''' (+ umlaut) || '''Cessative''' || '''nïzedik''' || '''forget (stop knowing)'''
|-
| lu- || [[Resumptive]] || luzedik || remember (resume knowing)
|-
| ko- || [[Almost]] || kozoduk || almost learn
|}

== See also ==

* [[Cess]] (redirect)
* [[Inchoative]]
* [[Resumptive]]
* [[Repetitive]]

[[Category:grammatical aspects]]
[[Category:Stative aspects]]
